title: APIv2 pods top of non-exist pod gets two response value
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The return value from API v2 pods top command of a non-exist pod is not the same as the docs. It include two response number 404 and 500.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start a podman APIv2 service
```
# podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:80
```
2. Use curl to try to get a non-exist pod's process
```
# curl -s -v -X GET "http://localhost:80/v1.40/libpod/pods/fakename/top"
*   Trying ::1:80...
* connect to ::1 port 80 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:80...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 80 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/pods/fakename/top HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2020 10:15:12 GMT
< Content-Length: 204
< 
{"cause":"no such pod","message":"no pod with name or ID fakename found: no such pod","response":500}
{"cause":"no such pod","message":"no pod with name or ID fakename found: no such pod","response":404}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Get two response:
{"cause":"no such pod","message":"no pod with name or ID fakename found: no such pod","response":500}
{"cause":"no such pod","message":"no pod with name or ID fakename found: no such pod","response":404}


**Describe the results you expected:**
only get one response 404

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Using upstream master (HEAD: 1fcb6788a5d7471a7ca6215a40e36e21812a0f6e) with a fedora 32 OS.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# podman info --debug

  arch: amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan I have not made any progress on this issue.
